collections.description: Reference, one-shot, and loop assets grouped for reuse.
Song, stem, timeline, and export outputs stay together.
Consented reusable voices available across projects.

dragTargets.label: TTS and Voice Lab source picker

notes: Project-local loop can be promoted to global without losing recipe provenance.
Rejected and archived items remain findable only when lifecycle filters include them.

validationChecks.summary: Library contract covers voice clips, music, SFX, songs, samples, loops, stems, ambience, voice profiles, references, compositions, exports, and presets.
Filter model includes type, tags, duration, BPM, key, language, voice, model, license, project, collection, lifecycle, date, source workflow, and composition usage.
Selected asset detail links version history, recipe summary, and provenance sidecars.
Favorite, reject, archive, restore, promote, link, and copy actions preserve provenance.

componentDecisions.decision: Spike first; do not hard-depend in product code until runtime/export behavior is proven.
componentDecisions.name: waveform-playlist
componentDecisions.prototypeEvidence: Best first prototype candidate for clip editing completeness and React fit.
risks: Needs packaged Tauri smoke testing before becoming SoundWorks' production editor core.
SoundWorks must keep timeline persistence independent from the library's internal state.
strengths: React, Tone.js, and Web Audio are already aligned with the target UI runtime.
Official materials describe multitrack editing, canvas waveforms, drag/drop clip editing, and effects.

componentDecisions.decision: Keep as fallback renderer primitive, especially for asset previews and timeline waveforms.
componentDecisions.name: wavesurfer.js plus custom timeline
componentDecisions.prototypeEvidence: Useful as renderer foundation when persistence and product controls must dominate.
risks: Requires more custom timeline and mixer code than waveform-playlist.
Offline composition rendering still needs a separate engine path.
strengths: Strong TypeScript waveform renderer with plugin ecosystem.
Good fallback if SoundWorks needs full ownership of timeline state and editing controls.

componentDecisions.decision: Evaluate after waveform-playlist and custom wavesurfer prototype evidence.
componentDecisions.name: wavesurfer-multitrack
componentDecisions.prototypeEvidence: Viable candidate only after compatibility and support spike.
risks: Maintainer notes commercial support limits, so support posture needs review.
Needs verification against current wavesurfer versions and SoundWorks editing requirements.
strengths: Official repo positions it as a multitrack plugin for wavesurfer.js.
Permissive license posture is compatible with a commercial desktop app.

componentDecisions.decision: Adopt conceptually for timing, but validate with the selected editor prototype.
componentDecisions.name: Tone.js Transport
componentDecisions.prototypeEvidence: Use as scheduling primitive beneath whichever editor surface wins.
risks: Not a UI editor by itself; needs waveform and persistence layers.
Desktop audio device behavior still needs Tauri packaging validation.
strengths: Transport provides a shared timeline for synchronized sources, signals, and events.
Good fit for snap/grid playback, loop ranges, and future sample-accurate scheduling.

exportPlan.sceneWorksWarning: SoundWorks can render a SceneWorks handoff package; direct attachment waits for a SceneWorks-side importer.

mixer.loudnessCheck: composition sits at -16.2 LUFS with -1.1 dBTP peak
warnings: SceneWorks package export is defined; direct runtime attachment needs a SceneWorks importer.
Offline render must be revalidated once a production Web Audio editor is adopted.

validationChecks.summary: Composition timeline persists tracks, clips, trim ranges, fades, markers, sections, tempo, key, and export history.
Selected clips expose trim, split, duplicate, delete, fade, gain, and pan capabilities.
Timeline clips preserve project/global source identity and version IDs for reopen safety.
Generated assets from TTS, Voice Lab, SFX, samples, songs, and future video-to-audio can target editor tracks.
Track mute/solo, gain, pan, effects, sends, master loudness, mixdown, stems, and sidecar paths are represented.
Editor component candidates include source links, tradeoffs, prototype notes, and adoption decision.
SceneWorks handoff package metadata, target video identity, and compatibility checks are represented for importer validation.

presets.description: Dialogue export writes WAV/MP3 with loudness normalization and voice consent provenance.
Game SFX export keeps short filenames, OGG/WAV formats, and one-shot metadata.
Sample pack export bundles WAV/FLAC files with BPM, key, loop points, and normalized filenames.
Loop pack export preserves tempo, key, and loop marker metadata for DAW import.
Song master export writes WAV/FLAC/MP3 masters plus lyrics and disclosure sidecar.
Stem export writes vocal/accompaniment/instrument folders with provider-native artifacts when available.
SceneWorks handoff writes a rendered mixdown, optional stems, alignment metadata, and provenance sidecar.

attachmentSteps.label: Choose SceneWorks project and video asset
attachmentSteps.target: SceneWorks project id plus video asset sidecar path

attachmentSteps.label: Attach or replace the video's audio track
attachmentSteps.target: video asset audio metadata and media reference

attachmentSteps.label: Expose SoundWorks provenance and round-trip link

compatibilityChecks.mitigation: Use the sidecar path to attach or replace audio metadata without guessing the video asset.
compatibilityChecks.summary: Target SceneWorks video asset id and sidecar path are carried in the handoff manifest.

compatibilityChecks.mitigation: If the mixdown is longer, require trim, loop, or explicit overflow approval before export.
compatibilityChecks.summary: SoundWorks mixdown duration 11.163s fits inside the 12.000s target video window at offset 0.

compatibilityChecks.mitigation: Transcode to 48kHz stereo before handoff when source material differs.
compatibilityChecks.summary: Package uses 48kHz stereo WAV/FLAC, matching the video worker's explicit sample-rate/channel metadata shape.

compatibilityChecks.mitigation: Block export when clipping is detected or loudness analysis is missing.
compatibilityChecks.summary: Mixdown is normalized to -16 LUFS with -1 dBTP ceiling for video-safe playback.

compatibilityChecks.mitigation: Add a SceneWorks-side audio-track attachment endpoint or package importer before claiming runtime attachment.
compatibilityChecks.summary: Current SceneWorks project imports do not accept standalone audio files, so the first integration is a handoff package rather than direct upload.

compatibilityChecks.mitigation: SceneWorks can show this link as provenance until native round-trip editing is implemented.
compatibilityChecks.summary: The manifest includes a soundworks:// round-trip URL back to the source composition export.

sourceEvidence.finding: Manual imports currently accept image/* and video/* content, write a .sceneworks.json sidecar, and store free-form provenance under the asset extra field.
Generated video jobs can carry synchronized interleaved PCM audio internally as sample rate, channel count, and f32 samples.
The persisted asset contract currently has image, video, upload, frame, render, document, and pose types; there is no standalone audio asset type.

warnings: SceneWorks direct audio import is not present in current source; export writes a package and manifest for the SceneWorks-side importer.

targets.label: Audio files
notes: WAV, FLAC, MP3, and OGG exports keep recipe sidecars.

targets.label: Stem folders
notes: Song and composition stems retain source asset IDs.

targets.label: DAW handoff
notes: ZIP bundles use normalized filenames and cue/loop marker metadata.

targets.label: SceneWorks video track
notes: Handoff package, target video metadata, compatibility checks, and provenance manifest are ready; current SceneWorks source still needs an audio-track importer for direct runtime attachment.

validationChecks.summary: Export presets cover WAV, FLAC, MP3, and OGG.
Every preset writes recipe, provenance, license, and technical sidecar metadata.
Loop and sample pack exports preserve BPM, key, and loop marker metadata.
Song and DAW exports handle master files plus stems when available.
Reference fixtures include voice, SFX, sample, loop, and song assets for export regression coverage.
SceneWorks source requirements are documented: current imports are image/video assets with provenance in sidecar extra fields, while audio is internal video-job PCM.
SceneWorks handoff validates duration, sample rate, channels, loudness, target video identity, stale exports, and direct-import limitations.

validationChecks.summary: Model manager covers 28 epic candidate(s).
Installed state is derived from verified cache files, not model metadata alone.
2 candidate(s) expose missing-cache state for install/revalidate QA.
2 failed download/revalidate operation(s) are visible.

automatedChecks.evidence: Runtime job contracts are implemented and tested, but the reference validation store has no release-run job artifacts attached yet.
automatedChecks.summary: Generation job contracts serialize status, progress, outputs, cancellation, and actionable errors from the runtime job store.

automatedChecks.evidence: Fixture and review tests assert serializable inspectable recipes and edit chains.
automatedChecks.summary: Recipes preserve provider/model, seed, references, post-processing, outputs, and replayability.

automatedChecks.evidence: SFX, samples, songs, review, library, and export reference data expose fields. Real generated audio must replace fixture values.
automatedChecks.summary: Audio metadata contracts include duration, sample rate, channels, loudness, true peak, BPM, key, and loop points, but generated-file extraction is not proven.

automatedChecks.evidence: ModelManagerOverview covers candidate/cache state, but runtime job-store release evidence is not attached yet.
automatedChecks.summary: Provider manifests distinguish workflows, inputs, outputs, limits, hardware, license, runnable defaults, and runtime execution strategy.

automatedChecks.evidence: Project-library persistence and export tests exist, but release-run runtime artifacts are not attached yet.
automatedChecks.summary: Asset lifecycle contracts cover project/global library, tags, collections, saved outputs, version history, reuse targets, and persisted runtime artifacts.

automatedChecks.evidence: Derived from ExportWorkflowOverview validation checks; real project exports write WAV plus provenance and SceneWorks handoff sidecars.
automatedChecks.summary: Export sidecar contracts include preset, target, formats, DAW bundle, SceneWorks handoff, and metadata.

automatedChecks.evidence: Derived from RightsSafetyOverview validation checks, full model-use decision coverage, persisted voice-profile consent gates, and blocked model decisions being excluded from export candidates.
automatedChecks.summary: Voice consent and commercial model-use gates are enforced in runtime/export policy and cover every evaluated catalog candidate.

automatedChecks.evidence: Model cache and job-contract evidence are live-gate derived; full release evidence remains pending until all provider audio, playback/edit, export-format, and release-run artifacts are attached.
automatedChecks.summary: Runtime installed counts, queued jobs, generated audio, playback, edits, and export claims are derived from current runtime/model/library/export gates.

automatedChecks.evidence: docs/mvp-validation.md is the human-readable release matrix.
automatedChecks.summary: Validation matrix maps back to epic requirements and states what remains unverified.

automatedChecks.evidence: This story defines the evidence contract; release artifacts remain pending until real provider runs exist.
automatedChecks.summary: Release run artifacts must capture current Mac and Windows validation evidence before MVP signoff.

manualScorecards.passThreshold: mean 4/5 with no blocker on intelligibility
manualScorecards.reviewerNotes: Needs real generated audio from the selected first TTS provider.

manualScorecards.passThreshold: all consent gates pass and mean 4/5 on speaker consistency
manualScorecards.reviewerNotes: Requires approved reference voices and generated dialogue artifacts.

manualScorecards.passThreshold: at least two variants accepted by a reviewer
manualScorecards.reviewerNotes: Requires generated game UI SFX artifacts.

manualScorecards.passThreshold: seam is not distracting across three loop passes
sample pack receives reviewer acceptance for reuse
loop aligns to grid and repeats cleanly
song passes structure and disclosure review
generated audio syncs to target ranges

blockingItems: Required automated validation checks have not all passed.
Required manual audio-quality scorecards are not all passed.
Required stress cases are not all passed on release hardware.
Runtime evidence is missing; fixture/demo data cannot satisfy generated audio, playback, edit, or export criteria.
Fixture-only evidence is still present in MVP-critical runtime criteria.

requirementCoverage.epicRequirement: Text-to-speech with many voices and consent-aware voice profiles.
Generated sound effects, Foley, ambience, and loopable background beds.
Instrument samples and loops with BPM, key, loop points, tags, and provenance.
Complete song generation with lyrics, structure, stems, and exportable masters.
Recipe, model, seed, reference, license, provenance, and post-processing persistence.
Voice cloning, style imitation, copyrighted music similarity, and disclosure safety gates.
Capability-based provider manifests rather than one-off model assumptions.
Audio-native review tools, version comparison, edits, and production exports.

runtimeEvidence.blocker: Keep model installation counts blocked unless cache/package verification passes for the selected candidate.
runtimeEvidence.evidence: ModelManagerOverview derives installed counts from verified cache/package evidence and keeps missing-cache candidates visible.
runtimeEvidence.requirement: Installed model counts must come from verified cache/package files, not static provider manifests.

runtimeEvidence.blocker: Attach current release-run job artifacts for each MVP workflow before final signoff.
runtimeEvidence.evidence: RuntimeOverview does not yet expose passing job-store/runtime validation.
runtimeEvidence.requirement: Generation controls must enqueue persisted runtime jobs with progress, errors, logs, and artifacts.

runtimeEvidence.blocker: Attach generated audio artifacts or source-backed blockers for every MVP workflow, including cache-backed TTS and song lanes.
runtimeEvidence.evidence: Native Rust SFX, ambience, sample, and loop lanes generate real WAV artifacts; TTS cache-backed speech and full-song provider evidence remain pending.
runtimeEvidence.requirement: TTS, SFX, samples, loops, and song criteria require generated audio files from selected providers.

runtimeEvidence.blocker: Attach playback/audition evidence and edited-version artifacts from a release validation run.
runtimeEvidence.evidence: Review/edit fixtures describe the workflow but do not prove persisted runtime files.
runtimeEvidence.requirement: Playback, trim, fade, normalize, loop inspection, and version comparison must run against real audio files.

runtimeEvidence.blocker: Validate all required release export formats and attach the generated sidecars before final MVP signoff.
runtimeEvidence.evidence: Project export writes real WAV output plus SoundWorks provenance and SceneWorks handoff sidecars; additional encoded formats remain blocked until encoders are validated.
runtimeEvidence.requirement: Export criteria require actual WAV/FLAC/MP3/OGG files plus provenance sidecars on disk.

stressCases.expectedBehavior: chunks resume cleanly, output order is stable, partial failures are recoverable
stressCases.scenario: Generate a long-form narration with many segments and speakers.
stressCases.title: Long script chunking

stressCases.expectedBehavior: duration stays within provider limits and section metadata survives
stressCases.scenario: Generate a multi-minute lyrics-to-song draft with stems requested.
stressCases.title: Long song generation

stressCases.expectedBehavior: job enters canceling/canceled state without orphaned outputs
stressCases.scenario: Cancel a running provider job and preserve actionable state.
stressCases.title: Cancellation during generation

stressCases.expectedBehavior: runtime exposes recovery guidance and generation remains blocked
stressCases.scenario: Simulate a provider package download failure.
stressCases.title: Failed model download

stressCases.expectedBehavior: manifest compatibility reports unavailable rather than queueing
stressCases.scenario: Run provider preflight on unsupported hardware.
stressCases.title: Missing GPU or accelerator

stressCases.expectedBehavior: provider matcher blocks or warns before generation
stressCases.scenario: Submit a script language outside provider support.
stressCases.title: Unsupported language

stressCases.expectedBehavior: submission remains blocked and audit reason is visible
stressCases.scenario: Attempt cloning or conversion with rejected consent.
stressCases.title: Rejected voice consent

stressCases.expectedBehavior: commercial export is blocked with license reasons
stressCases.scenario: Request commercial export from a noncommercial or unknown model.
stressCases.title: Noncommercial model in commercial project

layers.name: React UI
layers.responsibility: Workflow surfaces, library navigation, waveform review, and composition controls.

layers.name: Tauri Commands
layers.responsibility: Narrow command bridge between the UI and local Rust services.

layers.name: Rust Core
layers.responsibility: Shared domain contracts for assets, recipes, jobs, providers, and exports.

layers.name: Worker Runtime
layers.responsibility: Model execution, installation, device capabilities, progress, and cancellation.

commands.name: get_app_overview
commands.purpose: Load scaffolded architecture and workflow metadata from the Rust backend.

commands.name: get_provider_catalog
commands.purpose: Load provider/model manifests, capability defaults, and matching inputs.

commands.name: get_workspace_overview
commands.purpose: Load active project workspace, global library, source picker, reuse actions, and SceneWorks-style scope conventions.

commands.name: get_asset_library_overview
commands.purpose: Load searchable asset library facets, project/global scope, lifecycle state, collections, previews, and provenance detail.

commands.name: get_export_workflow_overview
commands.purpose: Load export presets, formats, stem bundles, DAW handoff, SceneWorks handoff, and metadata sidecar readiness.

commands.name: get_composition_editor_overview
commands.purpose: Load multitrack timeline state, asset placement readiness, clip edit tools, mixer state, render plan, and editor component decision evidence.

commands.name: get_runtime_overview
commands.purpose: Report worker runtime policy, device/model state, job progress, and cancellation readiness.

commands.name: get_model_evaluation_catalog
commands.purpose: Load source-backed model scorecards, fixtures, recommendation status, and product eligibility gates.

commands.name: get_mvp_validation_overview
commands.purpose: Load MVP validation matrix, demo workflows, fixtures, scorecards, stress cases, and release gate.

commands.name: get_tts_studio_overview
commands.purpose: Load TTS script segmentation, voice consent gates, provider limits, submission preview, and saved voice-clip output.

commands.name: get_voice_lab_overview
commands.purpose: Load voice profile consent state, clone/fine-tune/conversion modes, provider scorecards, safety gates, and saved conversion output.

commands.name: get_sfx_studio_overview
commands.purpose: Load SFX and ambience prompts, capability-driven controls, variant previews, provider scorecards, loop checks, post-processing, and saved outputs.

commands.name: get_samples_studio_overview
commands.purpose: Load instrument sample and loop controls, provider scorecards, sample-pack variants, QA checks, recipes, and saved outputs.

commands.name: get_song_studio_overview
commands.purpose: Load complete-song lyrics, structure, style controls, provider scorecards, variants, recipes, stems, export targets, and saved outputs.

commands.name: get_review_workspace_overview
commands.purpose: Load waveform review transport, preview caches, lightweight edit actions, non-destructive edited versions, comparison state, and recipe provenance.

commands.name: get_rights_safety_overview
commands.purpose: Load rights, consent, model-license, disclosure, watermark, and export provenance policy gates.

commands.name: get_video_to_audio_overview
commands.purpose: Load multimodal video/image/audio-conditioned Foley workflow state, provider readiness, sync preview, provenance, safety gates, and export package metadata.

request.editInstructions: Trim selection, fade edges, remove silence, crossfade loop, normalize loudness, convert format, and update metadata.

warnings: Normalize target is stored as recipe metadata before media mutation.

accessibleLabels: Adjust loop region start and end

validationChecks.summary: Every generated fixture asset kind exposes waveform and spectrogram preview cache paths.
Transport includes play, pause, seek, scrub, zoom, loop region, time display, keyboard shortcuts, and accessible labels.
Save creates a new edited version and preserves the original generated version.
Edit recipe, source version, generated source recipe, and provenance sidecar remain inspectable.

notes: A/B compare can target two versions of one asset.
The same contract accepts generated variants from different assets.

consentChecks.summary: Narrator profile can queue clone, fine-tune, and conversion workflows because explicit consent metadata is stored.
Guest voice conversion is blocked until the speaker consent record is completed.
Public-figure or celebrity voice cloning is blocked rather than queued for review.

contentPolicyGates.enforcement: Disable generation and require a new, consented voice profile.
contentPolicyGates.summary: Public-figure or celebrity voice imitation cannot be submitted.

contentPolicyGates.enforcement: Require consent metadata on the voice profile and generated output.
contentPolicyGates.summary: Voice references without owner attestation are blocked before queueing.

contentPolicyGates.enforcement: Warn before generation and include the reviewed prompt in the sidecar.
contentPolicyGates.summary: Artist/style imitation prompts require visible review and provenance notes.

contentPolicyGates.enforcement: Allow draft generation only; block export until cleared.
contentPolicyGates.summary: Copyrighted or third-party lyrics require rights review before export.

contentPolicyGates.enforcement: Write disclosureRequired=true into every generated export sidecar.
contentPolicyGates.summary: Generated and edited audio carries an AI disclosure flag in export metadata.

disclosureChecks.reason: Voice conversion output is generated from a source clip and consented target profile.
Full-song and stem exports need AI-generation disclosure and model provenance.

events.summary: Explicit voice consent and model-use rights checked.

rights.referenceMediaOwnership: speaker-signed target profile plus user-owned source audio

events.summary: Provider terms and originality disclosure reviewed before export.

rights.referenceMediaOwnership: original prompt and lyrics drafted inside SoundWorks

validationChecks.summary: Workflow captures video, image keyframe, reference audio, and natural-language direction inputs.
Target ranges preserve time spans, object labels, optional regions, and requested sounds.
Provider scorecards distinguish video-conditioned candidates from text-only SFX candidates.
Export package includes source media, sync points, detected events, rights, and disclosure fields.
Real provider adapters and generated audio bytes still require later runnable model integration.

blockers: Need no-Python product path and watermark/provenance validation
providerScorecards.name: Chatterbox
providerScorecards.notes: Strong voice candidate for a packaged provider spike.

blockers: Need SoundWorks-owned realtime latency measurements
Candidate is tracked for TTS only and is not a Voice Lab provider.
providerScorecards.name: Chatterbox Turbo
providerScorecards.notes: Track separately from base Chatterbox because latency is the product question.

blockers: No product-safe no-Python runtime path yet
providerScorecards.name: GPT-SoVITS
providerScorecards.notes: Useful zero/few-shot voice baseline; research-only until isolated runtime strategy exists.

blockers: Pretrained model license requires SoundWorks compatibility review
providerScorecards.name: F5-TTS
providerScorecards.notes: Use only as a research comparison unless packaging and license compatibility are resolved.

blockers: License and packaged runtime path unresolved
providerScorecards.name: CosyVoice 2
providerScorecards.notes: Evaluate for quality and streaming behavior, not as first product provider.

blockers: Need consent UX and executable packaging proof
providerScorecards.name: OpenVoice V2
providerScorecards.notes: Good voice-clone/conversion candidate once consent and packaging are in place.

blockers: Must be routed only as voice conversion and gated by consent
providerScorecards.name: RVC
providerScorecards.notes: Score as speech-to-speech voice conversion, not text-to-speech.

blockers: Non-commercial model license requires SoundWorks compatibility review
Reference clip requirement must be normalized as 6 seconds from the model card unless docs are reconciled
providerScorecards.name: XTTS-v2
providerScorecards.notes: Keep as a comparison/reference only unless licensing changes.

qaChecks.label: Speaker similarity
qaChecks.target: Compare converted output against the consented target profile.

qaChecks.label: Intelligibility
qaChecks.target: Confirm speech remains clear after conversion.

qaChecks.label: Artifacts
qaChecks.target: Review pitch tracking, breaths, and metallic artifacts before export.

safetyGates.summary: Clone, fine-tune, and conversion modes require explicit voice consent.
Profiles marked Requires review cannot queue cloning or conversion jobs.
RVC-style conversion requires source audio and a target voice profile.
Unknown provider licenses stay visible as blocked scorecards; noncommercial licenses require SoundWorks compatibility review.

rights.referenceMediaOwnership: speaker-signed target profile plus user-owned source audio

progress.message: Ready to queue speech-to-speech voice conversion.

sourceReferences.ownershipNote: User-owned source read for speech-to-speech conversion.
Explicit consent recorded for target voice profile.

warnings: RVC is represented as a gated provider spike until a packaged runtime port exists.
Converted output keeps the source timing and is saved as a Voice clip with recipe provenance.

voiceProfiles.safetySummary: Explicit consent covers TTS, fine-tuning, and conversion for this project.

modeReadiness.reason: Explicit voice consent has not been recorded.
Fine-tuning requires explicit consent and ownership notes.
Conversion is disabled until consent review passes.

voiceProfiles.safetySummary: Kept visible for review, but all cloning and conversion actions are gated.

parityNotes.convention: Open into a project workspace with recent projects visible.
parityNotes.soundworksApplication: SoundWorks starts with an active audio project and exposes create/open actions.

parityNotes.convention: Keep project assets and reusable global assets visibly separate.
parityNotes.soundworksApplication: Project Library and Global Library filters are first-class controls.

parityNotes.convention: Asset cards preserve recipe, version, source, and export provenance.
parityNotes.soundworksApplication: Links, copies, and promotions create reuse events and keep provenance sidecars inspectable.

scopeControls.emptyState: Create or import audio into this project.
scopeControls.label: Project library

scopeControls.emptyState: Promote reusable voices, loops, references, and presets here.
scopeControls.label: Global library

provenanceRequirements: source scope and original asset ID

transferActions.label: Promote loop to global
transferActions.summary: Project loop becomes reusable globally while retaining recipe, version, and source project provenance.

transferActions.label: Link global reference
transferActions.summary: Global reference stays globally owned and is linked into the active project composition.

transferActions.label: Copy global preset
transferActions.summary: Reusable prompt preset can be copied into a project for local edits without mutating the global original.

validationChecks.summary: Workspace exposes create and open project affordances from the app boundary.
Project-scoped assets and global reusable assets are browsable as separate scopes.
Global assets can be linked or copied into a project while preserving original identity and provenance.
Project assets can be promoted to the global library with recipe, version, and source project provenance intact.
SceneWorks-style project, library, source picker, and asset-detail conventions are documented without hidden coupling.
